nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Ayataka is a lightly processed Japanese RTD green tea with no added sugar. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry for unsweetened ready-to-drink green tea (FDC ID 171917) and cross-referenced with Coca-Cola Japan product label data. Ayataka contains approximately 0 kcal per 100 ml, consistent with unsweetened RTD green tea. Minor catechin and vitamin C content noted on product label.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of beverages: unsweetened green tea — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: Unsweetened green tea contains no glycemic carbohydrates. No direct GI entry exists for Ayataka specifically. GI is set to 1 (effectively zero) per convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages, consistent with University of Sydney GI Database guidance for non-caloric beverages. GL per serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (approximately 2 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Ayataka Green Tea is suitable for individuals with hypertension due to its negligible sodium content; regular consumption of unsweetened green tea has been associated with modest reductions in blood pressure in epidemiological studies.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates; no added sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ayataka Green Tea contains no fermentable carbohydrates or added sugars and has a glycemic index effectively of zero, making it a safe beverage choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes; catechins in green tea may also support insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, and presence of green tea catechins
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ayataka Green Tea contains no fat, no cholesterol, and no added sugar; the catechins (EGCG) present in green tea have been associated with cardioprotective effects including modest reductions in LDL cholesterol in clinical studies.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ayataka (綾鷹) is a popular Japanese ready-to-drink (RTD) bottled green tea produced by Coca-Cola Japan, first launched in 2007. It is brewed using a traditional Japanese method inspired by temple tea preparation, using coarser-ground tea leaves to produce a slightly cloudy, full-bodied green tea with a mild umami flavour and gentle astringency. The beverage is unsweetened and contains no added sugar, artificial flavours, or preservatives beyond vitamin C (ascorbic acid) as an antioxidant stabiliser. Per 100 ml, Ayataka provides essentially zero calories, zero protein, zero fat, and zero carbohydrates, making it a calorie-free hydration option. It contains trace amounts of sodium (approximately 2 mg per 100 ml) and potassium (approximately 14 mg per 100 ml). The tea is rich in green tea catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), which are associated with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardioprotective properties. Clinically, Ayataka is appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease due to its zero glycemic load, negligible sodium, and absence of saturated fat. It is sold in Singapore through Korean restaurant chains and convenience outlets, typically in 500 ml bottles priced between SGD 2.90 and SGD 3.00.
common_user_queries: Is Ayataka green tea good for diabetics?
Can I drink Ayataka if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Ayataka is a well-known commercial product with publicly available product label data. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central unsweetened RTD green tea and cross-referenced with Coca-Cola Japan product label. No exact USDA Branded Food entry was available for Ayataka Singapore SKU; therefore confidence is medium rather than high.
notes: Ayataka is a Japanese RTD green tea brand sold in Singapore at Korean restaurants including Ajumma's and Dapur Penyet. The input cuisine tag 'Korean' reflects the selling context (Korean restaurant menus on field survey data) rather than the product's Japanese origin; food_name and cuisine have been corrected to reflect the product's true identity as a Japanese beverage. energy_kcal is naturally zero as this is an unsweetened zero-calorie green tea beverage. protein_g is naturally zero as this beverage contains no protein by formulation. fat_g is naturally zero as this beverage contains no fat by formulation. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as this beverage contains no fermentable carbohydrates or added sugars. sugar_g is naturally zero by product formulation. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero for a liquid beverage. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per product formulation. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source for this beverage category. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. field survey source URL retained as provenance for restaurant context only; nutrient data sourced from USDA FoodData Central and Coca-Cola Japan product label.
